what_it_does: Renders a design-system preview card for DM Sans body typography, including sample body copy, alphabet glyphs, numerals, symbols, font weights, optical sizing, and usage metadata.
observations: The file is a standalone HTML preview page with inline CSS and a linked shared stylesheet named _card.css.
A dsCard metadata comment declares group, name, subtitle, and viewport values.
The card uses a two-column grid: specimen copy on the left and glyph samples on the right.
Typography is styled through CSS variables such as --font-body, --fg-primary, --fg-muted, and --fg-secondary.
The metadata row lists DM Sans, weights 400 through 700, optical sizing 9-40, and body usage.
standouts: The preview combines typographic hierarchy, glyph coverage, and font metadata in one compact card.
The dsCard comment makes the page catalogable without visible UI text.
The sample copy includes both product-language content and a URL preview string.
conventions: Uses semantic paragraph elements for displayed text specimens.
Keeps component-specific styling inline while relying on shared card tokens from _card.css.
Uses compact utility-like class names for typography size and role.
Encodes design catalog metadata in an HTML comment before the document root.
concerns: The visual behavior depends on CSS variables and _card.css that are not defined in this file.
The optical sizing statement is present as visible metadata but not enforced in the local CSS.
notes: A compact design-system specimen page for the DM Sans body type role.
questions: Are the dsCard comment fields consumed by a preview indexing tool?
Where are --font-body and the color variables defined?
Is optical sizing 9-40 descriptive metadata or intended to map to font-variation settings?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated card purpose describes a body typography preview with glyph specimen, and the file renders that content directly.
intention_signals: It appears intended to document and preview the default body font treatment in a design-system card format.
novelty: The @dsCard metadata comment encodes catalog grouping, display name, subtitle, and viewport directly alongside the preview markup.
contribution: A reusable typography-specimen card pattern that contributes DM Sans body-role documentation, glyph display, sample copy, and font metadata to a fused design ledger.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML design-system preview card showing DM Sans body typography specimens, glyphs, weights, optical sizing, and default UI usage metadata.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for cataloging and visually validating the body font treatment as part of a design-system handoff preview set.
